skill.name: error-debugging-error-trace
skill.description: Set up comprehensive error tracking and monitoring systems. Configure Sentry and similar services, implement structured logging, and create alerting workflows to quickly identify and resolve production issues.
skill.summary: Error tracking and observability expert for implementing monitoring solutions
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 35 static findings are false positives. The backtick characters flagged are JavaScript template literals, not shell execution. Network calls are legitimate error tracking API communications. Environment access patterns are configuration objects, not secret exfiltration. This is a legitimate error monitoring implementation guide.

medium_findings.title: JavaScript Template Literals Misidentified as Shell Execution
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer incorrectly flagged JavaScript backtick characters as Ruby shell backtick execution. Backticks in this code are used for template literals (string interpolation), a standard JavaScript feature. No shell execution occurs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: JavaScript template literals are clearly used for string interpolation (e.g., `${variable}`). No shell execution or backtick command substitution present.

medium_findings.title: Network Calls Are Legitimate Error Tracking
medium_findings.description: Fetch API and SSE connections are used for sending error events to configured error tracking endpoints. This is the core intended functionality of an error monitoring skill, not data exfiltration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network calls send error events to configured endpoints (e.g., Sentry DSN). This is standard error tracking behavior, not unauthorized data exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Configuration Access Misidentified as Environment Variable Access
medium_findings.description: The 'this.config' pattern flagged as env_access is a configuration object, not process.env. Additionally, the sensitive key sanitization is a security feature that prevents logging secrets.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Code accesses this.config which is a configuration object. The sensitive keys list is for sanitization (security feature), not for extracting secrets.

low_findings.title: Codebase Analysis Patterns
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged code pattern analysis as 'system reconnaissance'. This is the intended functionality - analyzing error handling patterns in a codebase for monitoring implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Analyzing error handling patterns is the core purpose of this error tracking skill, not malicious reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: Production errors go unnoticed without proper monitoring. This skill helps you set up Sentry and other error tracking systems to detect, triage, and resolve issues before users complain.
actual_capabilities: Set up Sentry SDK for Node.js, Python, and web applications
Configure error grouping, severity levels, and triage workflows
Implement structured logging with appropriate context
Create alert rules and notification routing for different error types
Add custom error fingerprinting for better deduplication
limitations: Requires application code access for SDK integration
Cannot retroactively capture errors before SDK installation
May require paid plans for advanced features on hosted services
Does not replace manual code debugging for complex issues

use_cases.title: Add error tracking to a new Node.js API
use_cases.description: Set up Sentry from scratch for a REST API, configure source maps, and create alerts for 5xx errors

use_cases.title: Improve existing error handling
use_cases.description: Analyze current error patterns, implement structured logging, and reduce noise from unactionable errors

use_cases.title: Create observability dashboard
use_cases.description: Build a real-time error monitoring dashboard with trend analysis and team alerting workflows

prompt_templates.title: Basic Sentry Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up Sentry error tracking for my Node.js application. The project is in /app and uses Express. Configure it to capture unhandled rejections and exceptions with environment 'production'.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick setup for a new project

prompt_templates.title: Configure Error Grouping
prompt_templates.prompt: Our Sentry dashboard has too many duplicate errors. Set up custom fingerprinting to group similar errors together based on the error message and stack trace pattern. Show me how to configure this in the SDK.

prompt_templates.title: Alert Routing Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure Sentry alerts for our team. Route critical errors (5xx responses) to Slack #incidents, warnings to #engineering, and rate limits to #alerts-oncall. Use environment-based rules.

prompt_templates.title: Structured Logging Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Migrate our console.log statements to structured logging compatible with Sentry. Include request ID, user ID, and operation name in the context for every log statement.

output_examples.input: Set up Sentry for my Express API
output_examples.output: I will set up Sentry error tracking for your Express API. This involves installing the SDK, initializing it with your DSN, and configuring it to capture unhandled exceptions and promise rejections.
I will create alert rules that notify your team via Slack when critical errors occur. This includes setting up environment-specific rules and defining severity thresholds.

best_practices: Always include user context (ID, email) when available to help debug user-specific issues
Use environment-based DSN configuration to separate production from development errors
Implement release tracking to correlate errors with specific deployments
anti_patterns: Capturing full request bodies without sanitizing sensitive fields like passwords
Setting sample rates too high during high-traffic periods causing bill spikes
Ignoring duplicate errors without proper fingerprinting configuration

faq.question: What languages and frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill covers Node.js, Python, and web JavaScript. Examples use Sentry but concepts apply to other services like Rollbar and Bugsnag.

faq.question: Do I need a Sentry account?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a Sentry account and project DSN. The free tier covers 5k errors per month which is sufficient for small projects.

faq.question: Can this skill help with performance monitoring?
faq.answer: Yes, the implementation playbook includes performance monitoring setup with tracing and profiling configurations.

faq.question: How do I handle sensitive data in errors?
faq.answer: Use the beforeSend hook to sanitize events. The skill includes examples that filter password, token, and secret fields.

faq.question: Does this work with self-hosted error tracking?
faq.answer: Yes, you can use self-hosted Sentry or any service compatible with the Sentry SDK protocol by pointing to your own DSN.

faq.question: How do I debug errors that Sentry is not capturing?
faq.answer: Check that the SDK is initialized before other middleware, verify the DSN is correct, and ensure sample rates are not set to 0.
